titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Wilfred M. Voynich and Ethel Voynich Provenance and Research files on the Cipher (Voynich) Manuscript
Voynich (Wilfred M.) Voynich and Ethel Voynich Provenance and Research files on the Cipher (Voynich) Manuscript
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Mary Caldera from catalog description by Barbara Shailor

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: Boxes: A, B, C, D, E, F, G, I, J, K, L, M, N (Oversize)
This collection contains 13 published digital objects. And those objects are comprised of 874 digital files.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday October 15, 2024 at 03:02

did.unittitle: Wilfred M. Voynich and Ethel Voynich provenance and research files on the Cipher (Voynich) manuscript

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains correspondence, notes, and articles on the provenance and research on the Cipher (Voynich) manuscript.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Wilfred M. Voynich and Ethel Voynich Provenance and Research Files on the Cipher (Voynich) manuscript. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Given to the Beinecke Library in 1969 by H. P. Kraus who had purchased it from the estate of Ethel Voynich.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Wilfred M. Voynich (1865-1930) was an antiquarian and rare book dealer. He acquired the Cipher manuscript, now known as the Voynich manuscript , in 1912. The undeciphered manuscript and accompanying files were inherited by his wife Ethel Voynich in 1930. Wilfred and Ethel Voynich conducted research on the manuscript's provenance and meaning.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Associated material: Cipher manuscript (Voynich manuscript) (Beinecke MS 408), Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The material is arranged as received.

did.unittitle: Johannes Marcus Marci of Cronland, autograph letter

descriptivenote.p: Johannes Marcus Marci of Cronland, autograph letter

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph letter of Johannes Marcus Marci of Cronland in which he presents the manuscript to Athanasius Kircher in Rome, in the belief that Kircher would be able to decipher it. "Reuerende et Eximie Domine in Christo Pater. Librum hunc ab amico singulari mihi testamento relictum, mox eundem tibi amicissime Athanisi ubi primum possidere coepi, animo destinaui: siquidem persuasum habui a nullo nisi abs te legi posse. Petijt aliquando per litteras ejusdem libri tum possessor judicium tuum parte aliqua a se descripta et tibi transmissa, ex qua reliqua a te legi posse persuasum habuit; uerum librum ipsum transmittere tum recusabat in quo discifrando posuit indefessum laborem, uti manifestum ex conatibus ejusdem hic una tibi transmissis neque prius huius spei quam uitae suae finem fecit. Verum labor hic frustraneus fuit, siquidem non nisi suo Kirchero obediunt eiusmodi sphinges. Accipe ergo modo quod pridem tibi debebatur hoc qualecunque mei erga te affectus indicium; huiusque seras, si quae sunt, consueta tibi felicitate perrumpe. retulit mihi D. Doctor Raphael Ferdinandi tertij Regis tum Boemiae in lingua boemica instructor dictum librum fuisse Rudolphi Imperatoris, pro quo ipse latori qui librum attulisset 600 ducatos praesentarit, authorem uero ipsum putabat esse Rogerium Bacconem Anglum. ego judicium meum hic suspendo. tu uero quid nobis hic sentiendum defini, cujus fauori et gratiae me totum commendo maneoque. Reuerentiae Vestrae. Ad Obsequia Joannes Marcus Marci a Cronland. Pragae 19 Augusti. AD 1666 [or 1665?].

did.unittitle: Correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence between W. Voynich and Prof. W. R. Newbold concerning Newbold's supposed decipherment of the manuscript (1919-26). Correspondence between Anne M. Nills, executrix of the estate of Ethel Voynich, and the Rev. Theodore C. Peterson, dated 1935-61, concerning the provenance, dating and decipherment of the manuscript.

did.unittitle: Friedman, William F. (and his associates), Washington, D.C.
Garland, Herbert, correspondence
Manly, John M. and Edith Rickent correspondence
Notes, articles, and miscellany
Scrapbook of newspaper clippings concerning the cipher manuscript, compiled by Wilfred Voynich

descriptivenote.p: Scrapbook of newspaper clippings concerning the cipher manuscript, compiled by Wilfred Voynich

did.unittitle: Provenance and miscellaneous files

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Miscellaneous handwritten notes of W. Voynich.

did.unittitle: Ciphers
Bacon, Roger
Plant drawings, identifications by Ethel L. V Voynich

descriptivenote.p: Plant drawings, identifications by Ethel L. V Voynich

did.unittitle: Ethel L. Voynich 's Thorndike notes
Collation – various notes and versions
Provenance
Provenance - John Dee
Provenance – Tepenecz, Jacobus de

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs of finest page of cipher manuscript.

did.unittitle: Provence - Marcus Mari
Provenance – Prague archives
Miscellaneous material, including handwritten notes by A. Nills about the cipher, and her correspondence about the sale of the manuscript
Five notebooks handwritten by Ethel Voynich

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebooks contain notes on the identification of the plants, medicinal herbs and roots; miscellaneous notes by A. Nills listing some characters or combinations of characters as they appear in the manuscript.

did.unittitle: Lectures, pamphlets, reviews and articles concerning the manuscript
Lectures, pamphlets, reviews and articles concerning the manuscript
Lectures, pamphlets, reviews and articles concerning the manuscript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes the transcript of a seminar held in Washington D. C. on November 1976 entitled "New Research on the Voynich Manuscript."

did.unittitle: Lectures, pamphlets, reviews and articles concerning the manuscript
Miscellaneous correspondence between R. Brumbaugh and J. M. Saul (Paris) and J. Arnold (Oak Grove, Mo.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Miscellaneous correspondence between R. Brumbaugh and J. M. Saul (Paris) and J. Arnold (Oak Grove, Mo.). Handwritten transcription of ff. 89v-116r by R. Brumbaugh.

did.unittitle: Handwritten transcription of ff. 89v-116r by R. Brumbaugh
Articles from international newspapers and magazines concerning the announced sale by H. P. Kraus of the cipher manuscript
